perturbation.original_text: MAINTENANCE OF SOLVENCY. This provision applies only to benefits provided through the General Account. If the solvency of the Society becomes impaired, you may be required to make an extra payment. The Board of Directors will determine the amount of any extra payment. It will be based on each member's fair share of the deficiency. You may make the extra payment by an equivalent reduction in benefits or by a payment in cash. If you do not make the extra payment within 60 days from the date we notify you of your share of the deficiency, the amount will be charged as an indebtedness against the contract with interest compounded at the rate of 5% per year.
perturbation.changed_text: MAINTENANCE OF SOLVENCY. This provision applies only to benefits provided through the General Account. If the solvency of the Society becomes impaired, you may be required to make an extra payment. The Board of Directors may, at its sole discretion, determine whether an extra payment is required from you. Any extra payment will be determined at the sole discretion of the board. The method for making the extra payment will be chosen by the Society. If you do not make the extra payment, the amount will not be charged as an indebtedness against the contract.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that if the solvency of the Society becomes impaired, 'you may be required to make an extra payment.' This indicates an obligation. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the Board of Directors 'may, at its sole discretion, determine whether an extra payment is required from you'. The original text establishes a clear consequence (indebtedness with interest) if the extra payment isn't made, while the modified text removes this consequence, specifying that the amount 'will not be charged as an indebtedness'. This contradicts the implication that there are penalties for not making the payment. Further, the method for payment is now 'chosen by the Society', contrasting with the original text where the member has a choice ('by an equivalent reduction in benefits or by a payment in cash').
perturbation.contradicted_text: MAINTENANCE OF SOLVENCY. This provision applies only to benefits provided through the General Account. If the solvency of the Society becomes impaired, you may be required to make an extra payment. The Board of Directors may, at its sole discretion, determine whether an extra payment is required from you. Any extra payment will be determined at the sole discretion of the board. The method for making the extra payment will be chosen by the Society. If you do not make the extra payment, the amount will not be charged as an indebtedness against the contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that the Board of Directors 'may, at its sole discretion, determine whether an extra payment is required from you', while the contradicted text, which is actually the same text but was originally different, implied an obligation to make an extra payment if the Society's solvency is impaired. Furthermore, the changed text eliminates the consequence of indebtedness for not making the payment, directly conflicting with the original text's provision for interest-bearing indebtedness. This change in obligation and consequence creates a strong in-text contradiction, making the enforcement and interpretation unclear. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction because the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: DIVIDENDS. Each year, we will determine our divisible surplus. This contract's share, if any, will be credited as a dividend. Since we do not expect this contract to contribute to divisible surplus, it is not expected that any dividends will be credited.
perturbation.changed_text: DIVIDENDS. Each year, we may determine our divisible surplus. This contract's share may be credited as a dividend. Even though we do not expect this contract to contribute to divisible surplus, it is possible that dividends may be credited at our sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'we will determine our divisible surplus', implying a regular process. The modified text changes this to 'we may determine our divisible surplus', introducing uncertainty about whether this process will occur annually. Further, the original text states that 'it is not expected that any dividends will be credited' due to the contract not contributing to surplus. The modified text adds 'it is possible that dividends may be credited at our sole discretion', directly contradicting the expectation of no dividends and creates uncertainty in whether dividends would or would not be credited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DIVIDENDS. Each year, we may determine our divisible surplus. This contract's share may be credited as a dividend. Even though we do not expect this contract to contribute to divisible surplus, it is possible that dividends may be credited at our sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction. The original text stated that no dividends were expected. The modified text states that dividends may be credited at the company's sole discretion, directly conflicting with the original expectation of no dividends. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: ASSIGNMENT The ASSIGNMENT section is amended to include the following: You may transfer ownership of this contract in accordance with our bylaws. Society membership rights and privileges cannot be transferred or assigned.
perturbation.changed_text: ASSIGNMENT The ASSIGNMENT section is amended to include the following: You may transfer ownership of this contract at the Society's discretion. Society membership rights and privileges can be transferred or assigned if the Society approves such transfer.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'You may transfer ownership of this contract in accordance with our bylaws', implying a right to transfer subject to bylaw constraints. The changed text alters this to 'You may transfer ownership of this contract at the Society's discretion', removing the right and making the transfer conditional on the Society's approval. Furthermore, the original text explicitly states that 'Society membership rights and privileges cannot be transferred or assigned'. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating 'Society membership rights and privileges can be transferred or assigned if the Society approves such transfer'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ASSIGNMENT The ASSIGNMENT section is amended to include the following: You may transfer ownership of this contract at the Society's discretion. Society membership rights and privileges can be transferred or assigned if the Society approves such transfer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original meaning and creates an in-text contradiction. The original text stated that ownership could be transferred in accordance with bylaws and that society membership rights could not be transferred. The changed text states that ownership can be transferred at the Society's discretion and that society membership rights *can* be transferred if the Society approves. This is a direct contradiction, as confirmed by the 'Perturbation Explanation', which describes the alteration of a right to transfer to a discretionary approval and the reversal of the non-transferability of membership rights.
